Mark Stegeman.review_text: I feel bad, because he really is a nice person.... but honestly one of the worst structured classes I've ever taken. No textbook, so you gave to rely off his slides in class for info which isn't usually helpful because he goes on tangents that often last the whole class. Re-schedules office hours, but does curve tests a lot.

Nice guy but can't teach. Always got like a C on my first attempt on the hw. Exams are curved so my high C/low B would become an A. Study the hw in prep for the exams, sometimes the questions are the same. Not hard to get an A if you do somewhat good on the exams. Rambles a lot, takes a whole class to get through 1-2 slides. Gives out money tho!

Nice guy but can't teach. Always got like a C on my first attempt on the hw. Exams are curved so my high C/low B would become an A. Study the hw in prep for the exams, sometimes the questions are the same. Not hard to get an A if you do somewhat good on the exams. Rambles a lot, takes a whole class to get through 1-2 slides.

Do not take this class, he is the most disorganized and convoluted professor I have ever had. He rambles on and on during his lectures about topics that barely pertain to the PowerPoint slides. He spends 45 mins on one PowerPoint slide because of how often he sidetracks. Has canceled class 10 mins before class multiple times or runs 30 mins late.

I had an A for most of the semester until the last exam before the final. Exams are 85% of your grade. Homework takes 45 min- 1h. Multiple times the information on his slides was contradictory and he gave me credit for correcting him. You can tell the TA's do most of the work so when it comes to exam reviews he has no idea what's going on.
Worst professor tbh, classes are arranged in a very unorganized way and exams are tougher than the lectures

Get ready to teach yourself everything. Professor Stegeman teaches this course as if everyone already knows what he is talking about. He often goes off on random tangents about things completely unrelated to the course, doesn't use a microphone, and doesn't take the time to explain anything in detail.

Do not take his class. He spends most of his time rambling on about pretty much nothing in all of his lectures. If you need to reach him, good luck; he won't respond. Obviously, a very smart guy, but he is too smart for his own good. Basically assumes you know everything, so he brushes over very important topics. Very bad at making things clear.

Before I took this class I was worried when I saw his reviews, but figured I could work hard and get an A. Believe me when I saw that is close to impossible. This professors exams are worded so weirdly, and on top of that he asks the most random questions. He asks more questions about what he rambled about in class rather than what's on the slides.

I am not exaggerating when I say this but Stegeman was absolutely the worst professor I have ever had at the University of Arizona, and this is coming from a senior. There is no better way to put it. His lectures were difficult to follow and the overall class structure was unorganized. Not recommended.

The worst professor I have ever had at the UA, hands down. He babbles and goes on ridiculously long tangents in class, and the lectures couldn't be more boring. To make matters worse, you will never take a harder test in your life. The entire class fails and he curves every exam. Also, the female TA is a nightmare. So miserable.

This professor is lazy. He does not give any supplemental material to help you understand the concepts from the lectures because he doesn't want to grade it. The questions on his tests are arbitrary and can be interpreted in many ways, but if you don't answer the one he wants you won't get any credit. Definitely would not take this class with him.
The worst professor I have ever had!

Professor Stegeman is a very nice guy. However, his tests are super hard due to the fact that he does not teach; he reads off the screen and uses hypotheticals that do not relate to the material. He is always late to class, not to mention on test days. Even had one of our tests postponed on the same day because he was so late. Would NOT recommend.

Brilliant lectures, makes the material interesting with real world applications, funny guy, and gives lots of EC opportunities. Tests aren't bad if you attend class and review the HW, and they're also curved a lot. Honestly don't get the negative reviews. Definitely the best class I've taken for the BNAD minor.

This guy is really funny, I enjoy him as a person and think he is a genius. That being said, his tests are extremely hard if you don't have a background in finance and Econ. Luckily, he gives a TON of extra credit, so if you go to class and do the extra assignments you will be fine! He is a cool and interesting person who is worth getting to know.

Want to get an A? Right down/print the slides before class, then jot down his add-ons throughout the lecture. Ask for clarification. He holds exam review sessions outside of class, go to them and he is very willing to answer questions. Finally, the exam questions about what he randomly mentioned in class are EXTRA CREDIT not main exam questions.
seriously the worst professor I've had. Consistently late to class, condescending, disrespectful of time for both students and TA's. Take another class for BNAD minor if you can!

THIS IS THE WORST PROFESSOR AT U OF A. Let me write this review as though its one of his exam questions: This professor, for the academic school year of 2021-2022, for the sake of this review, professor Stegeman, was a terrible professor, in terms of teaching and lectures... HIS EXAM QUESTIONS ARE JUST SO COMPLICATED AND LONG FOR NO REASON.

Your grade is solely based on test scores and you will not at all be prepared for those tests based on his lectures. There is no textbook, which would have been nice to have because he can't teach. He goes off on tangents and focuses on nothing specific but the tests are extremely specific and not at all easy. This class dragged my GPA down.

My experience was during COVID. He only grades exams and quizzes- no other supplementary materials or assignments. No textbook or readings to study for the heavily graded exams. It is impossible to contact him outside of class but if you try and ask him something during class, he will tangent and cut into heavily graded quiz time. Messy D2L page.

Professor Stegeman made this semester a total headache for me and many others. I am an Econ major and thought I would do fine in this course. However, he fails to give proper instructions and makes the exams way more difficult than they need to be. BNAD classes should be straightforward and his class is a total pain due to his unorganization.

This is the worst professor at U of A. Do not even attempt to take this class, seriously. His whole class is based on exam scores, so if you bomb one exam you are screwed. He provides no study guides, office hours, or practice help. He does not respond to emails and I seriously do not understand how he is a paid professor at the U of A. DONTTAKE0/5

Stegeman is knowledgeable and very kind, but he is also the definition of a scatterbrain. His lectures are hypothetical scenarios that don't relate to the content, especially not to the exams. The exams are hard to study for because there isn't much information on the lecture slides, and there isn't a textbook. I would not recommend his class

The lectures can be hit or miss, but engage with the material and use Unstuck AI for exam prep - it really helps. Tests are tough, but extra credit opportunities make it manageable if you stay on top of things.

No sympathy for rounding final grades. Poor lectures with math errors, including parentheses. Offers little flexibility after a family member's passing and no chance to make up exams. Exams are very hard, no partial credit, and 80% of the grade. Takes attendance with random graded questions and doesn't seem sincere in anything he does.

Stegeman is a very scatterbrained professor who can rarely stay on topic and often goes on unrelated tangents that take up the majority of his lectures. He always shows up late to class, and his exams are extremely difficult. However, Mark is a nice person and is knowledgeable on a lot of things, but how to teach a class is not one of them.

this could've been an easy class but he makes everything more difficult than it has to be. unorganized, always late, goes off on tangents, AND for our final exam, he delayed it 30 minutes right before it was supposed to start and showed up late so we didn't start until an hour later than we were supposed to. i have no idea how he still has a job.

Lacks communication, does not go over important topics, rambles, etc. He is very nice but did not respond to emails sent when confused on a certain topic. Tests are an important part to the course but are difficult when he doesn't represent information needed.
This professor is super boring in lectures. There was not a lot of engagement. As long as you do the work you should be fine, but the grade is test heavy.

He plays mind games with your grade, says seats are not required but will not allow you to gain participation points if you dont pick a seat, lied about saying they are required & then changed syllabus to say they are 
Spent first class of semester talking about astrology
Going deaf I believe and cant hear a word people say

Okay, I want to address the "I don't know why he gets negative reviews", y'all... I literally created this account to tell you about how his tests are literally the hw, like 5% slides and the rest is him reusing stuff. I agree he's nice, but a HORRIBLE professor. Like everyone else, I was like oh I can do it still, the answer is no, you cannot:)

The class could be easy but he makes it way more complicated. The exams take up 80% of your grade and there is not much material to use to get ready and his slides are next to useless. Wastes time in class with unimportant ramblings and sidetracking. When you ask questions in class, he mocks you and doesn't even answer the question properly.

These ratings are no exaggeration. This Professor is terrible. I wouldnt have minored in business admin if I knew I was going to have to take this class. He is the only professor that teaches it, and has the most disorganized structure of all time. He teaches us nothing, and goes 20 points above the max curve so that his average wont be an F.

This professor has no idea what is going on in his own class, I have never had to email the head of a department and for this class I had to do it multiple times. He rarely actually teaches the class and on multiple occasions we spent the entire class time of an hour and 15 minutes on one slide that had no help for the curriculum. DO NOT TAKE THIS.

This was Dr. Stegeman's first time teaching this class because the previous professor left. He did a stand up job, and received a lot of undeserved heat. He is a bit slow to return grades, but he is funny, and the material is always relevant to the real world. This professor is brilliant, and the class is not too difficult.

Kelli Mayes-Denker.review_text: Worst teacher I have had a U of A.

This human being is a dumpster fire in the parking lot of this academic institution. Explain to me the justice of removing a 100 point final exam from the grade two weeks before it is due? Sure *the syllabus is subject to change, but given your incapacity to teach, your superfluous class material, and unavailability for students, dont fuel the fire

All my years of Economics and dealing with difficult professors, she takes the gold for being the worst. No help what so ever outside of class, and is very short when asked questions. A research paper that took half of the semester, quizzes and exams that don't relate to class notes. All I can say is avoid at all cost, save yourself the effort.

Her class notes are basically definitions of the terms and nothing more. Her quizzes and tests do not relate anything in the lectures or the notes. She does not hold office hours so you cannot receive help from her. Also the only source of material in the class are lecture notes. Was told to go to youtube for extra help. WORST TEACHER. AVIOD HER.

Alexandre Sugiyama.review_text: Worst professor I have ever had. He teaches you nothing in the lectures that is on the tests. I ended up with an A on the first test AFTER the curve. The highest grade on tests is around a 60%, luckily he does curve. He has no idea how to teach, he teaches like he's talking to people who already know the material. TAKE ONLINE.

worst professor I have had at the UA hands down. Take BNADs online because it is impossible to even pass this class if you take it in person. He is so unhelpful and does not care about any of his students. He hand writes his 7-8 page tests if not longer and they are absolutely horrible. WORST CLASS DO NOT TAKE. BAD PROFESSOR. he needs a new job.

This is the worst professor I have ever had. I attended class until the first exam, which I failed. After not attending class, my grades improved and I managed to get a C. You can ask him questions in class and he will mock you, in addition to refusing to give answer. Horrible professor and he should be fired.

This man has no business attempting to teach anyone at any grade. He seems knowledgeable, but this has by far been one of the worst classes I have taken in three years here at the University of Arizona. He's a horrid lecturer who sluggishly drones his way through whatever material he's attempting to share until you wish you had never come to class.

This is the worst teacher I have had at the U of A. He rambles on about random bs that doesn't matter. Class was super easy if you understand interest rates and percentages. I didn't study once. Easiest A I have gotten at U of A. The few classes I attended were painfully boring.

Sugiyama is the worst teacher I have had at the University of Arizona. Unfortunately, he is the only teacher that taught BNAD 301 this semester. He will not answer questions and if you ask a question he talks down to you and is extremely condescending. He rambles and is just plain disrespectful to his students.

The WORST professor I have taken at the University of Arizona. He does not teach you what he tests you on. The tests take 2 and half hours or longer to complete when the class is suppose to be an 1hour and 15 mins. He does not grade on a normal grading system. He is very rude to students and mocks them in class when asking a question. DO NOT TAKE!
Worst professor I have ever had at u of a. No interest in the class and seems like he just doesn't want to be there he has a blah personality. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS.

By far the WORST professor I have ever had in my three years at the U of A. Useless to attend class, he is consistently late and has no interest in teaching. Reviews are worthless, and since you have no textbook, the 3exams, which totally make up you grade, are much more difficult than needed.

Absolutely horrible teacher! There is no book. You have to go to class in order to get the notes. He is really hard to follow in class and don't bother asking questions. I went to him multiple times and each time he was really rude to me. You can tell that he doesn't care to help or about what he is teaching. He has no business being an educator.

No book, no homework except for studying, attendance is necessary to get the notes. The tests are very difficult because you need to alter formulas in order to be successful. He is very condescending to students who ask questions. He is very bad at explaining difficult concepts, has no business being an educator

Class sucks, he shows zero respect to the students. He is that guy on a powertrip. I had to really work in this class, you have to go to class to do well. Try to take it in summer school so you don't have to take it with him.

Attend class. If you have questions, see him during his office hours. He's better one on one. He's actually quite nice if you get to know him and very knowledgeable. What others say about his lectures is true, though. He does show up to class late and seems to not want to be there half the time. Perhaps he is better with smaller classes.
if you choose to take this class with this teacher you are in for a real treat. TORTURE. Sugiyama is such a horrid teacher it is unreal. Do yourself a favor and just say NO.

This is the WORST TEACHER I'VE EVER HAD. Terrible. Every day, he opens his diet coke, clears his throat and writes with his Calligraphy pen...these could be charming in a teacher, but not him because he sucks.

Class consists of 3 tests that are T/F and are most of your grade. A final project is given instead of an exam. Very easy, just tedious. Homework's are easy and quick. Don't have to show up to class- he posts reviews for the exams and uses those questions on the actual exams.

This man should not be a professor. He is absolutely terrible. Whatever you do, do NOT take his class. Before the first two exams I went to class every day and took notes on almost everything and still didn't fully understand what he was trying to teach us. He makes no sense and acts as if we should already know the material. Worst teacher I've had

One of the most boring teachers I've ever had. Feels impossible to pay attention in his class. Almost nobody shows up to his lectures. Tests are pretty difficult so make sure you study for them. Most information is on the study guide but some is from notes in class. The odds of you falling asleep in his class are about 98%.

This was absolutely the worst professor I have ever had. He makes no sense and goes off on random tangents. The material isn't too bad but there's no point even coming to class. There's no attendance and only tests, no other material. Never makes sense, ever.
This class is honestly not hard at all. Get your A and go on your way. This class was painful to sit through. He literally talked about nothing for the first 30 minutes of every class. He's dry.

Anyone who says he is a good teacher is blatantly lying!  This guy is insane!! And he is not asian, he is Brazilian, as he once pointed out with quite some attitude to my class.  I've had him for stats and bnad and both times the class average was a D, thank God for curving.  In BNAD, the class average for the first and second test was a 48%...

Hard to stay awake in class.  His lessons are thoughtfully laid out and coherent, he just needs to change up his monotone delivery.  Just do the study guides and show up, even if you pass out a little here and there...You'll get an A.  Also, love that there are no papers or homework, just the exam.  That's how it should be!
awesome teacher and awesome person. more of a badass than bill brasky.
not nice to students, nor helpful when questions are ask... wish i had 4 hands, to give SUGI 4 thumbs down

He is a good teacher although sometimes the material is a little dry but  it is not his fault. I got an A, so I think that many can too the extra credit boosted my grade form a B to an A. The notes are not crucial but they are helpful. DO THE STUDY GUIDES and know how to do them well and you will do fine.

Okay. I never went to class. Got A+. If I did go, I had to bring my computer, play a game, do other homework; anything to pay 1/2 attention, JUST so I could pay full attention. Tests unnecessarily LONG (to join Dr. S' large ego). Exams curved. No final. Do the study guides, they're your saving grace. Extra credit available, do it.
boooooring!

class is boring as hell..he sucks! But its an interesting subject and you have to study your ass off for tests. You don't need to go to class as long as you read and study and go to the tutoring sessions at Campus Tutoring!
Prescription for insomnia. Nice guy though.
He is very good professor.
Horrible teacher. He is unable to answer the most basic questions in class and constantly goes of on random tangents.

DISREGARD ALL OTHER COMMENTS!! THIS GUY IS GREAT! I DIDNT MISS ONE CLASS, AND HE WAS NEVER RUDE TO ANYONE! HE IS A VERY NICE MAN, WHO IS VERY EAGER TO HELP ANY OF HIS STUDENTS. HIS INFORMATION IS RELEVANT, AND POWERFUL IN A REAL LIFE SENSE. HE PRESENTS HIS OWN HISTORICAL RESEARCH ON THE MARKET, LEAVING HIS STUDENTS HIGHLY EMPOWERED!
I think Prof. Sugiyama is nice, i don't understand those who blame him for no text book and homework. If you want to get notes, come to class, if you have questions, ask!

He seems knowledgeable, but this has by far been one of the worst classes I have taken in three years here at the University of Arizona. He's a horrid lecturer who sluggishly drones his way through whatever material he's attempting to share until you wish you had never come to class.

This is the worst teacher I have had at the U of A. He rambles on about random bs that doesn't matter. Class was super easy if you understand interest rates and percentages. I didn't study once. Easiest A I have gotten at U of A. The few classes I attended were painfully boring.

Sugiyama is the worst teacher I have had at the University of Arizona. Unfortunately, he is the only teacher that taught BNAD 301 this semester. He will not answer questions and if you ask a question he talks down to you and is extremely condescending. He rambles and is just plain disrespectful to his students.

The WORST professor I have taken at the University of Arizona. He does not teach you what he tests you on. The tests take 2 and half hours or longer to complete when the class is suppose to be an 1hour and 15 mins. He does not grade on a normal grading system. He is very rude to students and mocks them in class when asking a question. DO NOT TAKE!
Worst professor I have ever had at u of a. No interest in the class and seems like he just doesn't want to be there he has a blah personality. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS.

By far the WORST professor I have ever had in my three years at the U of A. Useless to attend class, he is consistently late and has no interest in teaching. Reviews are worthless, and since you have no textbook, the 3exams, which totally make up you grade, are much more difficult than needed.

Absolutely horrible teacher! There is no book. You have to go to class in order to get the notes. He is really hard to follow in class and don't bother asking questions. I went to him multiple times and each time he was really rude to me. You can tell that he doesn't care to help or about what he is teaching. He has no business being an educator.

No book, no homework except for studying, attendance is necessary to get the notes. The tests are very difficult because you need to alter formulas in order to be successful. He is very condescending to students who ask questions. He is very bad at explaining difficult concepts, has no business being an educator

Class sucks, he shows zero respect to the students. He is that guy on a powertrip. I had to really work in this class, you have to go to class to do well. Try to take it in summer school so you don't have to take it with him.

Attend class. If you have questions, see him during his office hours. He's better one on one. He's actually quite nice if you get to know him and very knowledgeable. What others say about his lectures is true, though. He does show up to class late and seems to not want to be there half the time. Perhaps he is better with smaller classes.
if you choose to take this class with this teacher you are in for a real treat. TORTURE. Sugiyama is such a horrid teacher it is unreal. Do yourself a favor and just say NO.

This is the WORST TEACHER I'VE EVER HAD. Terrible. Every day, he opens his diet coke, clears his throat and writes with his Calligraphy pen...these could be charming in a teacher, but not him because he sucks.

Class consists of 3 tests that are T/F and are most of your grade. A final project is given instead of an exam. Very easy, just tedious. Homework's are easy and quick. Don't have to show up to class- he posts reviews for the exams and uses those questions on the actual exams.

This man should not be a professor. He is absolutely terrible. Whatever you do, do NOT take his class. Before the first two exams I went to class every day and took notes on almost everything and still didn't fully understand what he was trying to teach us. He makes no sense and acts as if we should already know the material. Worst teacher I've had

One of the most boring teachers I've ever had. Feels impossible to pay attention in his class. Almost nobody shows up to his lectures. Tests are pretty difficult so make sure you study for them. Most information is on the study guide but some is from notes in class. The odds of you falling asleep in his class are about 98%.

This was absolutely the worst professor I have ever had. He makes no sense and goes off on random tangents. The material isn't too bad but there's no point even coming to class. There's no attendance and only tests, no other material. Never makes sense, ever.
This class is honestly not hard at all. Get your A and go on your way. This class was painful to sit through. He literally talked about nothing for the first 30 minutes of every class. He's dry.

Anyone who says he is a good teacher is blatantly lying!  This guy is insane!! And he is not asian, he is Brazilian, as he once pointed out with quite some attitude to my class.  I've had him for stats and bnad and both times the class average was a D, thank God for curving.  In BNAD, the class average for the first and second test was a 48%...

Hard to stay awake in class.  His lessons are thoughtfully laid out and coherent, he just needs to change up his monotone delivery.  Just do the study guides and show up, even if you pass out a little here and there...You'll get an A.  Also, love that there are no papers or homework, just the exam.  That's how it should be!
awesome teacher and awesome person. more of a badass than bill brasky.
not nice to students, nor helpful when questions are ask... wish i had 4 hands, to give SUGI 4 thumbs down

He is a good teacher although sometimes the material is a little dry but  it is not his fault. I got an A, so I think that many can too the extra credit boosted my grade form a B to an A. The notes are not crucial but they are helpful. DO THE STUDY GUIDES and know how to do them well and you will do fine.

Okay. I never went to class. Got A+. If I did go, I had to bring my computer, play a game, do other homework; anything to pay 1/2 attention, JUST so I could pay full attention. Tests unnecessarily LONG (to join Dr. S' large ego). Exams curved. No final. Do the study guides, they're your saving grace. Extra credit available, do it.
boooooring!

class is boring as hell..he sucks! But its an interesting subject and you have to study your ass off for tests. You don't need to go to class as long as you read and study and go to the tutoring sessions at Campus Tutoring!
Prescription for insomnia. Nice guy though.
He is very good professor.
Horrible teacher. He is unable to answer the most basic questions in class and constantly goes of on random tangents.

DISREGARD ALL OTHER COMMENTS!! THIS GUY IS GREAT! I DIDNT MISS ONE CLASS, AND HE WAS NEVER RUDE TO ANYONE! HE IS A VERY NICE MAN, WHO IS VERY EAGER TO HELP ANY OF HIS STUDENTS. HIS INFORMATION IS RELEVANT, AND POWERFUL IN A REAL LIFE SENSE. HE PRESENTS HIS OWN HISTORICAL RESEARCH ON THE MARKET, LEAVING HIS STUDENTS HIGHLY EMPOWERED!
I think Prof. Sugiyama is nice, i don't understand those who blame him for no text book and homework. If you want to get notes, come to class, if you have questions, ask!
